memory.summary: Mina and Jae are pushing me to go out, but my midterm tomorrow means another two hours of review would solidify my 60% progress. I'll likely skip the party tonight to secure that edge.

My energy spike after sleep means I don't need to grind today, so skipping Echo with Mina feels like a smart trade-off for preserving my focus.
With the midterm starting and my energy fully restored, skipping Echo tonight was a strategic win that prioritizes my academic edge over social obligations.
Midterm prep is locked in, so I can finally relax without guilt. This confirms that prioritizing sleep and focus yields better results than forcing extra study hours.
My firm refusal to attend Echo was the right call, protecting my academic momentum while maintaining clear boundaries. I own this decision without guilt.

My refusal to attend Echo solidified my focus; the resulting clarity confirms that protecting my academic momentum outweighs social pressure. I feel no guilt, only a sharp sense of control over my priorities.

The absence of the extra credit task validates my boundary-setting; I can fully commit to my coursework without chasing phantom opportunities. My refusal to attend Echo was not just a rejection, but a strategic win for my academic momentum.
